reasoning: The comparison value for 2026-03-29 (a Sunday) corresponds to Friday, March 27, which settled at 3.63. This is on the lower end of the recent baseline spread for SOFR (typically 3.64 to 3.65 in this inter-meeting period). The target date is April 5 (Sunday). Because April 3 is Good Friday (a SIFMA holiday), the last published SOFR prior to April 5 will be from Thursday, April 2. Thursdays normally see the rate hold at the baseline of 3.64 or 3.65 (e.g. March 26 was 3.65, March 12 was 3.65). Additionally, securing funding over the 4-day Easter weekend generally introduces firmer repo demand, making a rate drop unlikely. The combination of a depressed start value (3.63) relative to the baseline and positive calendar dynamics (Thursday print, long weekend demand) makes an increase to 3.64 or 3.65 the most likely outcome.
